info.title: Easy-Peasy.AI API
info.description:
OpenAPI Specifications for the Easy-Peasy.AI API.

All API requests must be authenticated with an API key. Include the `x-api-key` API key in the request header with all requests. You can get the API key [here](https://easy-peasy.ai/settings/api).

post.description: Programmatically generate outputs for any of the [Templates](https://easy-peasy.ai/presets). Each template (preset) accepts different parameters — see the request body schema and examples below.

Optionally filter by category. Only presets with input fields are returned.

post.description:
Retrieve the result of a transcription by its UUID. Since transcription is asynchronous (typically 1–5 minutes), poll this endpoint every 15–30 seconds until the `content` field is populated.

Once the transcription is complete, the response includes the full text, timestamped segments, and any previously generated AI content (summary, title, etc.).

audio.name: Team Meeting
audio.content: Speaker 1: Good morning everyone. Let's start with the project update...

post.description:
Generate AI-powered content from a completed transcription. Produces summaries, titles, action items, and more depending on the audio type.

**Content generated by audio type:**
- **Meeting**: summary, title, description, action items, keywords, timestamped overview, topics & bullets, LinkedIn post
- **Podcast**: summary, title, description, show notes, Twitter thread, article, newsletter, keywords, questions, LinkedIn post, timestamped overview, topics & bullets
- **Therapy Session**: summary, title, description, progress note, SOAP note, DAP note, keywords, timestamped overview, topics & bullets, LinkedIn post

You can generate all fields at once or specify individual fields with the `fields` parameter.

data.summary: The team discussed Q1 progress, reviewed the product roadmap, and assigned action items for the upcoming sprint.
data.title: Q1 Review & Sprint Planning Meeting
data.keywords: Q1 review, sprint planning, product roadmap, action items
data.action_items:
1. John to finalize the design mockups by Friday
2. Sarah to update the project timeline
3. Team to review the new feature spec

400.description: Bad request — audio not yet transcribed or invalid fields

example.error: Audio has not been transcribed yet

post.description:
Generate AI videos from text prompts or images using 40+ models including Veo 3.1, Kling 3.0, Sora 2, Hailuo, and more.

Video generation is asynchronous — the response returns a video ID immediately. Use the [Get Video](/api-reference/endpoint/get-video) endpoint to poll for the result.

**Input types:**
- **Text-to-video** — Provide only a `prompt`
- **Image-to-video** — Provide both `prompt` and `image` URL

**Note:** Video generation requires a paid plan.

value.prompt: A cat walking on the beach at sunset, cinematic lighting
The subject slowly turns and smiles at the camera

example.prompt: A cat walking on the beach at sunset

400.description: Bad request — invalid model, duration, aspect ratio, or input type

403.description: Plan limit reached or free plan

example.error: Video generation is not available on the Free plan. Please upgrade to a paid plan to generate videos.

get.description: Retrieve the status and result of a video generation request. Since video generation is asynchronous (typically 1–5 minutes), poll this endpoint every 15–30 seconds until the `status` is `completed` and the `url` field contains the video URL.

parameters.name: video_id
parameters.description: The video ID returned from the Generate Video endpoint

video.prompt: A cat walking on the beach at sunset
A cat walking on the beach at sunset

post.description:
Generate a talking video by animating a face image or video with speech. Provide either text + voice to generate audio, or supply your own audio file.

**Input combinations:**
- **Image + text + voice** — Generates speech from text using the specified voice, then animates the face in the image
- **Image + audio** — Uses the provided audio to animate the face in the image
- **Video + text + voice** — Generates speech from text, then lip-syncs the video
- **Video + audio** — Lip-syncs the video with the provided audio

**Models:**
- `premium` — High-quality avatar generation (VEED Fabric). Supports 480p and 720p.
- `standard` — Faster generation (WaveSpeed InfiniteTalk). Supports 480p and 720p.

Video generation is asynchronous. Use the [Get Video](/api-reference/endpoint/get-video) endpoint to poll for results.

**Note:** Requires a paid plan. Image must be at least 512x512 pixels. Audio/video max 5 minutes.

value.text: Hello! Welcome to our product demo.

example.prompt: Hello! Welcome to our product demo.

400.description: Bad request — missing required fields or invalid input

403.description: Plan limit reached or free plan

example.error: Talking video generation is not available on the Free plan. Please upgrade to a paid plan.

post.description:
Generate AI sound effects from a text description using ElevenLabs. Returns the audio URL immediately (synchronous).

Usage is counted against your plan's character limit.

value.prompt: A door creaking open slowly in a haunted house
Rain falling on a tin roof with distant thunder

example.text: A door creaking open slowly in a haunted house

400.description: Bad request — missing prompt or plan limit reached

get.description:
Retrieve available filter options for text-to-speech voices.

Returns a list of all available languages, accents, and total number of predefined voices.

This endpoint helps you understand what filter values you can use when calling the `/api/get-text-to-speech-voices` endpoint.

**Workflow:**
1. Call without parameters to get all available languages
2. Choose a language
3. Call with `language` parameter to get accents available for that language
4. Use language and accent to filter voices in `/api/get-text-to-speech-voices`

**Note:** This endpoint does not require authentication.

Retrieve a list of available voices for text-to-speech generation.

Returns all predefined voices (ElevenLabs and OpenAI) and optionally the user's custom cloned voices.

**Filtering:**
- Filter by language using the `language` query parameter
- Filter by accent using the `accent` query parameter
- Both filters can be used together

**Authentication:**
- Optional: Endpoint works without authentication for public voices
- Required for custom voices: Include `x-api-key` header

parameters.name: x-api-key
parameters.description: Your API key (optional, required for custom voices)

parameters.name: include_custom
parameters.description: Include user's custom cloned voices in the response (requires authentication)

example.error: Your account is blocked. Please contact support.

post.description:
Convert text to speech using various AI voices. The generated audio is returned as a publicly accessible URL.

**Usage Limits:**
- Free plan: Limited words per billing period
- Paid plans: Higher limits based on subscription tier

value.text: Hello! This is a test of the text-to-speech API. It supports multiple languages and custom voice settings.

400.description: Bad request - Missing parameters or usage limit reached

value.error: You have reached the limit of 10000 characters for your plan. Please upgrade your plan to continue using the service.

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing API key

value.error: Your account is blocked. Please contact support.

slug.description: Unique preset identifier to pass to `/api/generate`

description.description: Short description of what the preset does
description.example: Generate paragraphs with the click of a button!

category.description: Category or categories the preset belongs to

isFree.description: Whether this preset is available on the free plan

description.description: Short description of what the preset does
description.example: Generate paragraphs with the click of a button!

category.description: Category or categories the preset belongs to

isFree.description: Whether this preset is available on the free plan

fields.description: Input fields required by this preset. Pass these as body parameters to `/api/generate`.

name.description: Field name to use as the request body key in `/api/generate`

placeholder.example: example: how to optimize your website for search engines

maxLength.description: Maximum character length for the field value

default.description: Whether this option is selected by default

preset.description: The template name to use for generation. See [Templates](https://easy-peasy.ai/presets) for the full list.

keywords.description: The main input text or topic for the template.

tone.description: The desired tone of the output (e.g. `professional`, `friendly`, `funny`).

shouldUseGPT4.description: Whether to use the advanced AI model.

extra1.description: Additional context field. Usage varies by template (e.g. background information, key skills, blog intro).

extra2.description: Additional context field. Usage varies by template (e.g. blog post title, reply sentiment).

extra3.description: Additional context field. Usage varies by template (e.g. job title).

extra4.description: Additional context field. Usage varies by template (e.g. recommendation details).

suffix.description: End section text for the Fill the Gaps template.

prompt.description: The textual description of the image to be generated

model.description: The model to use for image generation

style.description: Style for the image (e.g. Cyberpunk, Watercolor). Not all models support this parameter.

artist.description: Artist style to emulate (e.g. Van Gogh). Not all models support this parameter.

useHD.description: Use HD quality. Supported by select models.

image.description: Image URL for Image-to-Image generations. Provide a source image to edit or transform.

resolution.description: Resolution quality level. Supported by FLUX.2, Nano Banana, and other select models.

recraftStyle.description: Style preset for Recraft v3 model.

waitForResult.description: Wait for the image to be fully generated before returning the response. If false, returns immediately with a pending status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the generated image

model.description: The model used for the image generation

used_credits.description: The number of credits used for the generation

prompt.description: The prompt used for image generation

audio_type.description: The type of audio (e.g., podcast, meeting)

language.description: The language of the audio (e.g., English, Chinese, French)

enhanced_quality.description: Whether to use enhanced quality for transcription

url.description: The URL of the audio file

uuid.description: Unique identifier for the created transcription

languages.description: List of all available languages (sorted alphabetically)

accents.description: List of available accents. If `language` parameter was provided, only accents for that language are returned.

total_voices.description: Total number of predefined voices available

filtered_by_language.description: The language filter that was applied, or null if no filter was used

filtered_voices_count.description: Number of voices available for the filtered language, or null if no filter was used

image.description: Full URL to voice avatar image

filters.description: Filters that were applied to the results

languages.description: List of available languages in the filtered results

accents.description: List of available accents in the filtered results

available.description: Available filter options based on current results

voiceID.description: Voice ID to use for generation (get IDs from the Get TTS Voices endpoint)

text.description: The text to convert to speech
text.example: Hello! This is a test of the text-to-speech API.

url.description: Publicly accessible URL of the generated audio file (MP3)

used_characters.description: Word count of the text (used for billing)

prompt.description: Text prompt describing the video to generate.
prompt.example: A cat walking on the beach at sunset, cinematic lighting

image.description: URL of an image for image-to-video generation. If omitted, text-to-video mode is used.

duration.description: Duration of the video in seconds. Available values depend on the model.

resolution.description: Video resolution (supported by some models like Veo 3.1).

generateAudio.description: Whether to generate audio along with the video (supported by some models like Veo 3.1, Kling 3.0).

id.description: Video ID. Use this to poll for the result with the Get Video endpoint.

image_url.description: Video URL. Empty string while processing.

url.description: Video URL. Empty string while processing, populated when complete.

status.description: Current status. `processing` means the video is still being generated. `completed` means the video URL is ready.

prompt.description: Text description of the sound effect to generate.
prompt.example: A door creaking open slowly in a haunted house

duration.description: Duration of the sound effect in seconds.

prompt_influence.description: How closely the output follows the prompt (0.0 = more creative, 1.0 = more literal).

url.description: URL of the generated audio file (MP3)

seconds.description: Duration of the generated sound in seconds

image.description: URL of a face image to animate. Must be at least 512x512 pixels. Provide either `image` or `video`.

video.description: URL of a video to lip-sync. Supported formats: .mp4, .mov. Duration: 3–300 seconds. Provide either `image` or `video`.

text.description: Text to convert to speech. Required if `audio` is not provided.

voiceID.description: Voice ID for text-to-speech. Get available voices from the [Get TTS Voices](/api-reference/endpoint/get-tts-voices) endpoint. Required if `text` is provided and `audio` is not.

audio.description: URL of an audio file to use directly (instead of generating from text). Max 5 minutes.

avatarModel.description: Avatar generation model. `premium` uses VEED Fabric (higher quality), `standard` uses WaveSpeed InfiniteTalk (faster). Only applies to image input.

generateCaptions.description: Whether to generate captions on the video.

audio_id.description: The UUID of the audio transcription (returned from Create Transcription)

content.description: Full transcription text. Empty string until processing completes.

segments.description: Timestamped segments with optional speaker detection

speaker.description: Speaker label (if speaker detection was enabled)

action_items.description: AI-generated action items (Meeting type, if generated)

show_notes.description: AI-generated show notes (Podcast type, if generated)

audio_id.description: The UUID of the audio transcription

fields.description: Specific fields to generate. If omitted, all fields appropriate for the audio type are generated.

force.description: If true, regenerate content even if it already exists.

data.description: Object containing the generated content fields. Keys depend on the audio type and requested fields.

apiKeyAuth.name: x-api-key
apiKeyAuth.description: API key for authentication. Get yours at https://easy-peasy.ai/settings/api
